title: Analyze Datetime Columns After Upload
summary: Parse dates correctly, filter ranges, and chart trends over time in Datilio.

content.summary: Parse dates correctly, filter ranges, and chart trends over time in Datilio.

body.text: Datetime errors are the silent killer of trend charts. Preview types before plotting anything over time. Upload and confirm date columns imported as dates, not text. Filter to your analysis window—last 90 days, fiscal YTD—using date range filters.

body.alt: Abstract visualization representing AI and data
body.caption: Abstract visualization representing AI and data. Photo: Unsplash

body.text: Upload and confirm date columns imported as dates, not text. Filter to your analysis window—last 90 days, fiscal YTD—using date range filters.
Check for timezone-less timestamps if events cross regions; document assumptions in rule names.

items: Watch for US vs EU date format confusion in CSV sources.
Exclude partial months when comparing MoM growth.
Use line charts for continuous time, bars for discrete periods.

body.text: Plot daily or weekly aggregates with line charts. Combine category filters to split trends by product or channel.
Save date-range rules for recurring monthly reviews on fresh uploads.
Datetime analysis in Datilio follows preview-filter-chart—never skip preview when dates drive the story.

meta_description: Parse dates correctly, filter ranges, and chart trends over time in Datilio.
